rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA MORTGAGE AND REALTY, INC. VS. RONALD W HERTEL et al

rulings.Case Title: FAR EAST NATIONAL BANK, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. DINESH MANIAR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Appointing Reciever And Preliminary Injunction; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 5, 2009, line 6, PLAINTIFF FAR EAST NATIONAL BANK, Motion For Order Appointing Receiver and Preliminary Injunction. Motion is granted. Defendant defaulted under the loans, the loan documents authorize the appointment of a receiver. Construction of improvements on the property have ceased, several mechanic liens are recorded on the property, and at least three complaints to foreclose mechanics liens have been filed. Defendant owes plaintiff approximately $6,880,000.00 and the property in its current condition is probably insufficient to discharge the debt. Plaintiff will suffer waste and irreparable harm without the appointment of a receiver and preliminary injunction. Damages not accertainable and would not afford adequate relief. Receiver to file a bond in the amount of $75,000.00. Plaintiff to file a bond in the amount of $75,000.00. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: HSBC BANK (USA) INC. VS. THOMAS MAYFIELD et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 5, 2009, line 4, DEFENDANT LOTTIE TAYLOR DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Defendant Taylor is not a former owner of the property. A purchaser of property, as sucessor to the "landlord," is subject to the San Francisco Rental Ordinance. Plaintiff failed to comply with the Rent Ordinance. San Francisco does not recognize a transfer of ownership through a foreclosure sale as a basis for eviction. Plaintiff failed to allege a proper basis for an eviction in the 3-day notice, and failed to plead an exemption from the Rent Ordinance. =(302/CWW)
